İstanbul, Ankara, İzmir ve il il AFAD son depremler.. 06 Mart 2026 deprem son dakika haberleri haberin detayında... Artçı deprem mi oldu? Son deprem büyüklüğü ne kadar? Yakınımdaki depremler nelerdir? Anlık deprem mi oldu? Son dakika canlı deprem Türkiye haritası.. Kandilli Rasathanesi ve Deprem Araştırma Enstitüsü ve AFAD deprem haberleri.. İstanbul'da deprem mi oldu? Ankara'da deprem mi oldu? İzmir'de deprem mi oldu? Hangi illerde deprem oldu ve hissedildi? Bugün deprem mi oldu? Deprem ne zaman ve kaç şiddetinde oldu? articles.title_tr: Kurtulmuş: BMGK'nin New York'taki bir kafeden farkı kalmadı
articles.summary: Meclis Başkanı Numan Kurtulmuş, rektörlerle iftar yemeğinde buluştu. Dünyadaki askeri gerilimler karşısında Birleşmiş Milletler Güvenlik Konseyi'nin (BMGK) etkisizleştiğini belirten Kurtulmuş, "BMGK'nin New York'taki bir kafeden farkı kalmamıştır." dedi.

articles.title: Marathon Players Report Keeping Server-Slam Loot In Full Game
articles.title_tr: Marathon Players Report Keeping Server-Slam Loot In Full Game
articles.summary: Bungie's Marathon is off to the races, and everyone was supposed to have a fresh start after the Server Slam beta test. None of the rare loot gained during Server Slam was meant to cross over into the game's launch, but for a handful of lucky players, that's exactly what happened.As demonstrated by an X user going by Nifty n00b (via Kotaku), the loot was waiting for them in the in-game mailbox. Marathon allows players to temporarily lend their items to other players in the game, which are then automatically returned to them through the mailbox. That's why a handful of players who lent out their loot in Server Slam may find that they still have their items if they check their mailbox.INSANE LOOT HACKIf you had rewards in your mailbox from the server slam, THEY ARE STILL THERE...24X Backpack ...Purple Core...before my first game 😂🤣 pic.twitter.com/e1Zoiwn7da  — Nifty n00b (@NiftyN00b) March 5, 2026              Since this fluke flies in the face Bungie's desire to restore parity after each season, it seems likely that the bug will be addressed or removed at some point in the near future. Earlier this month, Bungie justified that approach by saying, "It's a way to say goodbye to the old, welcome the new, and kick off a journey from zero-to-hero with new ways to play, content to master, and things to discover." That's also one of the reasons why Marathon's premium currency, LUX, can only be used on cosmetics, rather than giving players who pay extra an edge.Continue Reading at GameSpot

Earlier this month, Bungie justified that approach by saying, "It's a way to say goodbye to the old, welcome the new, and kick off a journey from zero-to-hero with new ways to play, content to master, and things to discover." articles.title: The Super Mario Lego Piranha Plant Is 30% For A Limited Time
articles.title_tr: The Super Mario Lego Piranha Plant Is 30% For A Limited Time
articles.summary: Lego Super Mario Piranha Plant (540 Pieces)                                                                                            $42 (was $60)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              See at Amazon                                                                                                                                                                           See at Target                                                                                                                                                                           See at Best Buy                                                                                                                                                                           See at Walmart                                                                    The Lego Super Mario Piranha Plant is down to its best price since last holiday season, as Amazon and Target are both discounting the popular set to just $42 (was $60). This is the most exciting deal we've seen in months--the set hit its all-time low of $35 last October, but since then has seen no meaningful price cuts. So if you're looking for a fun project to kill time before winter finally ends later this month, this affordable Piranha Plant could be a great choice for you or your family.                                                                                                        Lego Super Mario Piranha Plant (540 Pieces)                                                                $42 (was $60)                                                                                                                                                                                                                      Based on one of Mario's best-known enemies, the 540-piece Piranha Plant Lego building set has multiple posable elements, including an opening/closing mouth, a bendable vine, and two leaves that can be angled in different directions. The set also comes with two (fake) coins that can be hidden inside the Piranha Plant's mouth.Just like in the video games, the Piranha Plant Lego set has a buildable green warp pipe as its base. This is essentially an artificial potted plant for Nintendo fans. The completed Piranha Plant measures 9 x 4.5 x 6.5 inches.It's worth mentioning that the Piranha Plant is one of three Lego Mario display models that does not interact with the Mario, Luigi, and Peach figures from the Lego Super Mario and Mario Kart Starter Sets. The other two are Mario & Standard Kart and the replica Game Boy, both of which launched this year.                                                        See at Amazon                                                                                                                            See at Target                                                                                                                                                See at Best Buy                                                         Crafted to look just like its in-game counterpart, the Lego Super Mario Piranha Plant features a poseable head, mouth, stalk, and leaves. That makes it a bit more interactive than you'd expect, so you can change up its look from day to day depending on your mood. The plant sits inside a buildable pipe, and two coins are included as bonus accessories. It stands just over 9 inches tall and around 4.5 inches wide when completed.While it probably won't take more than an afternoon to complete, the finished product makes for an excellent display piece--it's a great size to toss on an end table, shelf, or anywhere in your game room. Keep in mind that this doesn't have any interactive digital features, so if you have one of the interactive Lego Mario figures, you won't get any compatibility between the two (though they'll certainly look cool placed side by side).Continue Reading at GameSpot

articles.title: One Punch Man Is Punching His Way Into MapleStory This Month
articles.title_tr: One Punch Man Is Punching His Way Into MapleStory This Month
articles.summary: One Punch Man's bald, bad-guy-fighting superhero, Saitama, is punching his way into MapleStory later this month. Nexon's pixelated 2D side-scroller MMO is hosting the MapleStory x One Punch Man Collaboration, a limited-time in-game event that begins later this month.The event will bring Saitama, his dedicated disciple Genos, Tatsumaki, Fubuki, King, and several other One Punch Man characters to MapleStory as playable Maple Heroes, and will allow players to take part in limited-time One Punch Man-themed quests and event missions.          In addition to new characters and quests, MapleStory players can also earn exclusive themed rewards and unlock special skills tied to anime's favorite Caped Baldy. More info on the event will be released on MapleStory's official website.Continue Reading at GameSpot

articles.title: Scientists discover a hidden force that helps wire the brain
articles.title_tr: Scientists discover a hidden force that helps wire the brain
articles.summary: Growing neurons rely on chemical cues to find their targets, but new research shows that the brain’s physical properties help shape those signals. Scientists discovered that tissue stiffness can trigger the production of guidance molecules through a force-sensing protein called Piezo1. This protein not only detects mechanical forces but also helps maintain the structure of brain tissue. The discovery reveals a powerful link between the brain’s physical environment and how its wiring is built.

articles.title: One Of My Favorite TTRPGs Is Kicking Butt On Kickstarter
articles.title_tr: One Of My Favorite TTRPGs Is Kicking Butt On Kickstarter
articles.summary: Dungeons & Dragons is practically synonymous with "tabletop role-playing games," but sometimes you want something a little different. Or at least I do. And in the wake of Wizard of the Coast's 2023 OGL fiasco, a ton of TTRPGs that are D&D-like but different in their own way popped up--Critical Role's Daggerheart, Kobold Press' Tales of the Valiant, The Arcane Library's Shadowdark, Gila RPGs' Dragon Slayers, and MCDM Productions' Draw Steel, just to name a few. My favorite of the lot (so far--time and money prevents me from playing everything I want to try) is Nimble, which originally existed as an alternative ruleset for D&D 5e in 2023, but then it evolved into its own system in 2024 with a Backerkit crowdfunder that exceeded its $5,000 goal by over $260,000. Now, Nimble is on Kickstarter for a reprint and expansion, and it has overshot its $50,000 goal by $663,000 with a week to go (as of publishing this article).Nimble is a system built on speed. It accomplishes this by being more efficient than a lot of other combat-driven TTRPGs. Just comparing it to D&D 5.5e, Nimble has four ability scores instead of six, and 10 skills instead of 18 (which is too many, WotC!). These changes make for a smaller and easier-to-track character sheet. This also makes it very easy to onboard new players, even if they've never played a TTRPG. Instead of keeping track of the pages of notes you'd need for something like D&D, Nimble only asks you to keep on top of what amounts to half a page of numbers when you're first starting out. In Nimble, you can understand your character at a glance.Combat is where Nimble really shines, though. There aren't a ton of weapons and spells but each does something unique and is useful, and since initiative puts emphasis on the players going before enemy creatures and attacks almost always hit, every player feels suitably heroic whenever combat happens. A simple and easy-to-remember action economy and loosy-goosey handling of initiative order ensures the Game Master can keep battles feeling punchy and fast-paced as well, controlling the tempo of every scene and creating avenues for the players to be both creative and tactical. My biggest qualm with D&D, especially at the higher levels of play, is that a single combat encounter can last for three to four hours (sometimes longer!). In Nimble, the longest encounter I've ever had was an hour. I like having room for the exploration and social pillars in my sessions, which typically only last for three hours.Continue Reading at GameSpot

articles.title: Pressure mounts on Tudor as Tottenham lose at home to Palace
articles.title_tr: Pressure mounts on Tudor as Tottenham lose at home to Palace
articles.summary: Tottenham stay just one point above the Premier League relegation zone following a 3-1 home defeat by Crystal Palace, having had to play more than 45 minutes with 10 men after Micky van de Ven was sent off.

articles.title: The Pokemon Company Pushes Back Against Trump Administration's Latest Pokemon Meme
articles.title_tr: The Pokemon Company Pushes Back Against Trump Administration's Latest Pokemon Meme
articles.summary: Following the Trump administration sharing a Pokemon Pokopia meme on X, Pokemon Company International has taken a stand against the White House's repeated unauthorized use of its property. Sravanthi Dev, a Pokemon Company spokesperson, addressed the post in an official statement given to The New York Times, writing:"We are aware of recent social content that includes imagery associated with our brand. We were not involved in its creation or distribution, and no permission was granted for the use of our intellectual property. Our mission is to bring the world together, and that mission is not affiliated with any political viewpoint or agenda."MAGA 🇺🇸⚡️ pic.twitter.com/8QRVP23zGu  — The White House (@WhiteHouse) March 5, 2026              The image shared by the White House reads "make america great again" written in the same font used in the latest Pokemon game, Pokemon Pokopia, and set on top a screenshot from the game. This is part of a larger trend, in which fans of the Pokemon series have used a font generator to create their own version of the game's logo with custom text.Continue Reading at GameSpot
